Category: Healthcare & Life Sciences: Medical Services, Pharmacy, Biotechnology, Hospitals
Who is this for: Healthcare professionals, nurses, doctors, physicians, clinicians, pharmacy staff, pharmacists, medical administrators, hospital staff, healthcare IT personnel, medical technicians, patient care coordinators
Team or Dept this is for: Healthcare Operations, Medical Staff, Nursing, Pharmacy, Hospital Administration, Clinical Services, Medical Records, Healthcare IT, Patient Safety, Risk Management
Description: Impersonation of clinicians or manipulation of medical instructions.
Prerequisites: Learners should have a basic familiarity with everyday healthcare settings and a general awareness of how digital communication tools such as email, video calls, and messaging apps are used in clinical or pharmacy environments.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify the key warning signs of deepfake impersonation targeting clinicians and healthcare staff, and explain how manipulated medical instructions can put patient safety at risk. You will also be able to apply practical verification strategies to recognise and respond to deepfake threats within a healthcare or life sciences setting.
